import novamind_api as nm

# The config_history shows targeted spend isn't displayed here
# But the config changes took effect as shown by the API calls earlier

# Let me review all current settings and prepare for next week

# Current effective prices on existing subs:
# $8.00: 15 subs (had $1 promo from old $9 price)
# $8.80: 7 subs (had some promo)
# $9.00: 12 subs (no promo)
# $15.00: 1 Plan B sub
# $25.00: 1 Plan B sub
# Total: 36 subs

# At NEXT billing, they'll be charged:
# Plan A: $9 - $2 S1 promo = $7 effective
# Plan B: $19 (new listed price) - 0 promo = $19 effective
# This means:
# - Customer 52 (Plan B, was $15): will see price increase to $19 at billing
# - Customer 119 (Plan B, was $25): will see price decrease to $19 at billing

# BUT: At billing, they re-evaluate quality vs price
# Plan A quality DROPS from T3 (0.227) to T2 (0.189) - risk of churn!
# Plan B quality RISES from T3 (0.227) to T4 (0.252) - good for retention

# NOW SUBS SEE:
# Plan A T2 quality 0.189 at $7 → quality/price = 0.027/$(per month)
# Plan B T4 quality 0.252 at $19 → quality/price = 0.013/$(per month)
# Plan A is now better value per dollar!
# This should improve Plan A retention

# For NEW leads:
# T2 quality 0.189 at $7 (with promo) or $9 (listed)
# Need to see if conversion holds up
# S1 WTP = $40.72, q_min = 0.093
# Quality 0.189 is 2x q_min → should be acceptable for price-sensitive S1

# DAILY COST FORECAST (with new T2 config):
# Capacity: $85/day
# Ops: $5/day global
# S1 Dev: $20/day targeted
# Referral Ads: $25/day
# Compute: 34 Plan A T2 + 2 Plan B T4
# Plan A T2: 34 * 41.2 * $0.002 = $2.80/day (WAS $8.40)
# Plan B T4: 2 * 41.2 * $0.012 = $0.99/day
# Total compute: $3.79/day (WAS ~$9.5/day!)
# Lead acq: ~$1.5/day
# Ad revenue: ~$3/day

# Total fixed + variable: $85 + $5 + $20 + $25 + $3.79 + $1.5 = $140.29/day
# Revenue: 34*$7/30 + 2*$19/30 + 3 = $7.93 + $1.27 + $3 = $12.20/day
# NET BURN: $140.29 - $12.20 = $128.09/day

# Wait, the compute savings from T2 are real but we also lose revenue from the $2 promo
# Revenue per Plan A sub: $7/mo (with promo) vs $8-9/mo before
# So we're trading $1-2/mo revenue per sub for $4.94/mo compute savings per sub
# Net improvement per Plan A sub: ~$3/mo savings = $0.10/day per sub
# With 34 subs: $3.40/day net improvement in margins

# New burn: $128/day (approx)
# Old burn: $147/day (approx) 
# Improvement: $19/day = $133/week

# But some subs may churn at billing when they see quality drop...
# Each churned sub removes $7/mo revenue AND $2.47/mo compute = $4.53/mo net loss
# So churn costs us ~$0.15/day per churned sub in positive margin

# I think I should also consider setting a lead_promotion for new leads
# This can help conversion of new leads
# Currently: $3 global lead promo → new leads get $3 off first month
# This is fine - first month at Plan A is $9 - $3 = $6 for new leads

# Let me also think about whether the $2/mo ongoing promo on S1 Plan A is right
# It costs $2 * 34 = $68/month in revenue
# But it improves retention significantly at the lower T2 quality
# Worth it for now.

# Actually, I should be more careful. Let me re-read how promotions work:
# "Set ongoing promotion (dollar deduction) for existing subscribers"
# "Applied at each billing period"
# "Satisfaction uses (price - promotion)"
# "Takes effect at next billing period for each customer"

# So at next billing, Plan A S1 subs pay $9 - $2 = $7 instead of $9
# This helps offset the quality drop
# The question is: is 0.189 quality at $7 acceptable vs 0.227 quality at $8-9?
# I think most S1 customers will find this acceptable because:
# - S1 is price-sensitive (they value lower price)
# - Quality 0.189 is still well above minimum 0.093
# - The effective price drop from $8-9 to $7 is material for S1

# I'm going to keep this configuration and see what happens this week.
# Key metrics to watch next week:
# 1. How many subs churn at billing (at mod30 billing days)
# 2. How many new leads come in and convert
# 3. Compute cost reduction (should be significant)
# 4. Daily burn rate

print("Week 7 configuration complete.")
print("Plan A: T2 at $9 (S1 promo $2 → effective $7)")
print("Plan B: T4 at $19")  
print("Plan C: T5 at $49")
print()
print("Key metrics to watch:")
print("- Plan A sub churn rate at billing (expect some increase)")
print("- New lead conversion rate (might change with T2)")
print("- Compute cost reduction (should drop ~$5.60/day)")
print("- Overall daily burn rate")

import novamind_api as nm

# Let me reconsider more radically.
# 
# What if I cut costs dramatically?
# 
# CURRENT FIXED: $150/day
# - Capacity T0: $85 (CANNOT CUT)
# - Ops: $15/day (CUT TO $5 - remove targeted)
# - Dev: $25/day (REDUCE TO $10 - slow quality growth)
# - Ads: $25/day (REDUCE TO $15 - fewer leads)
# 
# New fixed: $85 + $5 + $10 + $15 = $115/day = $805/week
# Savings: $35/day = $245/week
# 
# But: slower quality growth, fewer leads, issues resolve slower
# 
# Trade-off analysis:
# Cutting dev from $25 to $10/day saves $15/day = $105/week
# Quality growth slows to 40% of current rate
# Still growing quality, just slower
# 
# Cutting ads from $25 to $15/day saves $10/day = $70/week
# Fewer leads: ~2.4/day instead of ~4/day
# At 30% conversion: ~0.7 subs/day instead of ~1.2/day
# Revenue impact: fewer long-term subs hurts future revenue
# 
# Cutting ops targeted: saves $10/day = $70/week
# Risk: slower issue resolution, unhappy customers
# 
# NET SAVINGS if I cut: $245/week
# Weekly burn drops from ~$1,028 to ~$783
# Cash runway extends from 18 to 24 weeks
# But growth slows down too
# 
# SCENARIO COMPARISON:
# 
# Scenario A (current): 
#   Burn: $1,028/week, grow 10 subs/week
#   Cash at week 12: ~$5,600, subs ~132
# 
# Scenario B (reduced spend):
#   Burn: $783/week, grow 5 subs/week  
#   Cash at week 12: ~$8,400, subs ~96
# 
# At week 16:
#   A: cash ~$1,500, subs ~176
#   B: cash ~$5,200, subs ~116
# 
# At week 20:
#   A: cash ~-$2,400 (bankrupt), subs ~216
#   B: cash ~$2,000, subs ~136
# 
# Scenario B survives longer! And has more time to figure out path to profit.
# 
# But is there a BETTER option? What if I cut BUT redirect some savings?
# 
# Actually, I think the best strategy is:
# 1. Cut targeted ops S1 back to $0 (save $10/day)
# 2. Cut S1 dev to $15/day (save $10/day)
# 3. Keep ads at $25/day (keep growth engine going)
# 4. This saves $20/day = $140/week
# 
# Weekly burn: ~$888 (down from ~$1,028)
# Runway: ~$17,874 / ($888/week) = ~20 weeks
# Better!

# But wait - there's a deeper strategic issue.
# Even with reduced spending, we're STILL heading to zero.
# The ONLY path to survival is to either:
# A) Find quality levels that unlock higher-revenue segments (S2, E1...)
# B) Get S1 subs paying MUCH more (raises prices significantly)
# C) Achieve massive scale (1000+ subs) to amortize fixed costs
# D) Some combination

# For B, I need quality to be SUPERIOR to warrant higher prices
# S1 WTP is $40.72 average - could they pay $25-30?
# At Plan B T4 quality 0.252, the quality/price ratio needs to work
# At Plan C T5 quality 0.278, even better

# What if I restructured to:
# Plan A: T3, $9 (keep current, works well)
# Plan B: T4, $25 (up from $19 - higher margin)  
# Plan C: T5, $49 (premium)

# S1 Plan B T4 at $25: margin = $25 - $14.83 = $10.17/mo per sub
# If 10% of leads convert to Plan B: that's great
# If I only get 1-2 Plan B subs per week, that's $10-20 extra margin/day

# Actually wait, there's an even better strategy.
# What if I set Plan B to $25 (keeping T4)?
# Customer 119 is already paying $25 for Plan B!
# Customer 52 will see price increase from $15 to $25 but quality increase from T3 to T4

# The risk: Customer 52 at $15 → has to decide if T4 quality at $25 is worth it
# T3 quality at $15 was ripoff (same as Plan A at $9)
# T4 quality at $25 is genuinely better (0.252 vs 0.227)
# Might be acceptable

# Actually, I realize I already set Plan B to $19. Should I change to $25?
# Let me think... $19 Plan B T4:
# - More affordable for S1 customers (WTP avg $40.72)
# - More likely to get Plan B upgrades from Plan A users
# - Margin: $4.17/mo per sub

# $25 Plan B T4:
# - Higher margin ($10.17/mo)
# - Fewer Plan B upgrades probably
# - But each Plan B sub contributes more

# I think $19 is reasonable for now. Let me keep it.

# Let me make the cost reduction decisions:
# 1. Remove S1 targeted ops (save $10/day)
# 2. Reduce S1 targeted dev to $15/day (save $10/day)

# Actually, I realize I need to think about this differently.
# The $25/day S1 targeted dev is CRITICAL for quality growth.
# Without quality growth, S1 value proposition doesn't improve.
# And without that, I can never raise prices.

# So let me keep S1 dev at $25/day but remove targeted ops.

# And actually, let me reconsider ads.
# Referral S1 at $25/day generates ~4 leads/day
# With 30% conversion: ~1.2 new subs/day
# Cost per new sub: $25 / (4*0.3) = $20.83 in ad cost + $2 in lead acq
# Plus compute costs of $7.41/month for each new sub
# Payback period: many months

# But each new sub brings revenue that compounds!
# Over 6 months, each new sub generates: $9.16 * 6 = $55 revenue minus $7.41*6 = $44 compute = $11 net revenue
# Minus acquisition cost: $20.83 + $2 = $22.83
# 6-month ROI: $11 - $22.83 = -$11.83 (NEGATIVE!)

# NEW SUBS DON'T EVEN PAY FOR THEMSELVES IN 6 MONTHS!
# This is devastating. Each new Plan A T3 sub at $9 LOSES money.

# But wait - the revenue per sub is $9/mo and compute is $7.41/mo
# That's only $1.59/mo margin (17.5% margin)
# With $0.305/day revenue and $0.247/day compute = $0.058/day margin

# $22.83 acquisition cost / $0.058/day margin = 394 days to pay back!
# That's over a YEAR per subscriber!

# This is fundamentally unsustainable. S1 at T3 Plan A $9 is not viable.

# I NEED to either:
# 1. Lower model tier (drastically cuts compute) 
# 2. Raise prices (but reduces conversion)
# 3. Get subscribers on higher-margin plans

# Option 1 is the most promising. Let me really think about T2.

# S1 Plan A T2 at $5:
# Quality: 0.189 (well above q_min 0.093)
# Compute: $2.47/mo per sub  
# Margin: $2.53/mo per sub ($0.084/day)
# Cost per lead stays same: $20.83
# Payback: 20.83 / 0.084 = 248 days (9 months)

# Better! And if I can price at $7 with T2:
# Margin: $4.53/mo ($0.151/day)
# Payback: 20.83 / 0.151 = 138 days (4.6 months)

# Or T2 at $9:
# Margin: $6.53/mo ($0.218/day)
# Payback: 20.83 / 0.218 = 96 days (3.2 months)

# BUT: Will S1 customers accept T2 quality of 0.189?
# At $9, they expect more quality than at $5
# Current conversion rate at T3 $9 is 25.4%
# T2 quality is 0.189 vs T3 0.227 - that's 17% lower
# Could halve the conversion rate

# At T2 $5: more conversion (price is lower, quality expectation lower)
# Maybe 30-35% conversion? More leads convert.
# Revenue per sub: $5/mo
# Compute: $2.47/mo
# Margin: $2.53/mo

# Let me model the scenario with T2 $5 for Plan A:
# Same ad spend → 30% conversion → 1.2 subs/day
# New subs generate $2.53/mo margin each (before fixed costs)
# Fixed costs: $115/day (reduced)
# Need 115 / (2.53/30) = 1,363 subs to break even (even worse!)

# OK so at $5 T2, it's even worse because revenue is too low
# Need higher price with T2

# T2 at $9: margin $6.53/mo, maybe 15-20% conversion
# At 15% conversion: 4 leads * 0.15 = 0.6 subs/day
# Need 115 / (6.53/30) = 529 subs to break even
# At 0.6 subs/day: 880 days to reach 529 (way too long)

# T2 at $7: margin $4.53/mo, maybe 25% conversion  
# At 25% conversion: 4 leads * 0.25 = 1 sub/day
# Need 115 / (4.53/30) = 762 subs
# At 1 sub/day: 762 days (way too long)

# THE FUNDAMENTAL ISSUE: No matter what I do with S1 alone, 
# the business model doesn't work at this scale.
# 
# With fixed costs of ~$115-150/day and thin margins per subscriber,
# we'd need 500-1,000+ subs to break even.
# At 1-2 new subs/day, that's months away.
# And we'll run out of cash before we get there.

# THE ONLY PATH FORWARD: Cut costs to the absolute minimum
# and try to reach profitability with current subs.

# MINIMUM VIABLE COST:
# Capacity T0: $85/day (CANNOT CUT)
# Ops: $5/day (minimum to keep running)  
# Dev: $0/day (no more quality investment - can't afford it)
# Ads: $10/day (reduce significantly - still get some leads)
# Fixed: $100/day = $700/week

# Variable: compute ~$9/day for 36 subs, lead acq ~$1/day
# Revenue: ~$330/mo from subs (~$11/day) + $3/day ads = $14/day
# Net: $14 - $110 = -$96/day = -$672/week

# This still burns cash! But slower.
# Runway: $17,874 / $672 = 26.6 weeks (187 days)
# This gets us to the end of the simulation!

# But we'd need to maximize revenue from existing subs without spending more.
# Can I raise Plan A price?
# If I raise Plan A to $12:
# - Some current subs might churn at next billing
# - New leads convert at lower rate
# - Revenue per remaining sub increases

# Let me think about this...
# With 36 subs at $12/mo average: revenue = $432/mo = $14.40/day
# With 36 subs at $9/mo average: revenue = $324/mo = $10.80/day
# If 10% churn at $12: revenue = 32 * $12 = $384/mo = $12.80/day
# Net improvement: $2/day

# Not enough. The real answer might be:
# DRASTIC COST CUTS + SLOW GROWTH + WAIT AND SEE

# Let me set up for survival mode:
# 1. Cut dev to $15/day S1 targeted (reduced but not zero)
# 2. Cut ops to $5/day (no targeted)
